prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
In the thick of a eerie fog, lighthouse keeper Edmund trudged towards the source of a peculiar sound echoing through the mist. His curiosity piqued, he left his post, treading carefully over the slick rocks. The sound grew louder—a rhythmic clicking, unlike anything he'd heard in his decades by the sea.

A dark, hulking mass began to materialize from the fog. Edmund squinted, his heart pounding. It was a ship, unlike any he'd ever seen. Its brass bow gleamed even in the dull light, and enormous metal legs hung off its sides, jerking spasmodically, creating the clicking sound. A gargantuan steam-powered crab, lost in time and fog, had found its way to Edmund's lighthouse. His eyes widened, he stared in awe at the spectacle, his lighthouse a mere beacon of reality in this suddenly surreal world.
